sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 2025 회계연도 시놉시스는 Ansys 인수 관련 대규모 자금 조달과 운영 현금 흐름 개선을 통해 사업 확장을 추진 중입니다.

keyPoints.text: Ansys 인수 자금 조달을 위해 143억 달러 규모의 선순위 채권(Senior Notes)을 발행하며 재무 구조에 큰 변화가 발생했습니다.
기술 변화에 대응하기 위한 R&D 투자와 전략적 인수를 지속하고 있으며, 이는 제품 경쟁력 유지의 핵심 동인입니다.

riskChanges.text: 글로벌 시장에서의 핵심 인재 확보 경쟁이 심화되고 있으며, 보상 패키지 경쟁력 저하 시 인력 유출 위험이 존재합니다.
기후 변화, 팬데믹, 사이버 공격 등 예상치 못한 재난이 글로벌 인프라 및 운영 시스템을 마비시킬 잠재적 위험이 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: Ansys 인수 관련 지출로 인해 투자 활동 현금 흐름이 159억 달러 순유출을 기록하며 전년 대비 큰 폭으로 변동되었습니다.
규제 환경의 복잡성 증가로 인해 컴플라이언스 비용 및 관리 리소스 투입이 지속적으로 확대되고 있습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025 회계연도 매출은 70억 5,417만 달러, 순이익은 13억 3,222만 달러를 달성했습니다.
총자산은 482억 2,446만 달러이며, 현금 및 현금성 자산은 28억 8,803만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: Ansys 인수 후 통합 과정에서의 비용 효율화와 대규모 부채에 따른 이자 비용 부담이 향후 수익성 개선의 주요 변수가 될 것입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: FY2025 revenue = 70억 5,417만 달러 (USD 7.054B; 7,054 million), XBRL range 2024-11-01..2025-10-31. Readable Korean unit: 70억 5,400만 달러.
FY2025 operating income = 9억 1,492만 달러 (USD 0.915B; 915 million), XBRL range 2024-11-01..2025-10-31. Readable Korean unit: 9억 1,500만 달러.
FY2025 net income = 13억 3,222만 달러 (USD 1.332B; 1,332 million), XBRL range 2024-11-01..2025-10-31. Readable Korean unit: 13억 3,200만 달러.
FY2025 total assets = 482억 2,446만 달러 (USD 48.224B; 48,224 million), XBRL range instant..2025-10-31. Readable Korean unit: 482억 2,400만 달러.
FY2025 cash and cash equivalents = 28억 8,803만 달러 (USD 2.888B; 2,888 million), XBRL range instant..2025-10-31. Readable Korean unit: 28억 8,800만 달러.

Competition for these individuals and other qualified employees is intense and has increased globally, including in major markets such as Asia. Our employees are often recruited aggressively by our competitors and our customers worldwide. Any failure to recruit and/or retain senior management and key employees could harm our business, operating results and financial condition. Additionally, efforts to recruit and/or retain such employees could be costly and negatively impact our operating expenses. We issue equity awards from employee equity plans as a key component of our overall compensation.

We face pressure to limit the use of such equity-based compensation due to dilutive effects on stockholders. If we are unable to offer attractive compensation packages in the future, it could limit our ability to attract and retain senior management and key employees. We may pursue new product and technology initiatives or expand into adjacent markets, and if we fail to successfully carry out these initiatives, we could be adversely impacted. As part of the evolution of our business, we have made substantial investments to develop new products and enhancements to existing products through our acquisitions and research and development efforts. If we are unable to anticipate technological changes in our industry by introducing new or enhanced products in a timely and cost-effective manner, or if we fail to introduce products that meet market demand, we may lose our competitive position, ou

Our business is subject to evolving corporate governance and public disclosure regulations and expectations that could expose us to numerous risks. We are subject to changing rules and regulations promulgated by a number of governmental and self-regulatory organizations, including, among others, the SEC, the Nasdaq Stock Market, the Financial Accounting Standards Board, other federal agencies, states and the international governing bodies such as the European Union. These rules and regulations continue to evolve in scope and complexity making compliance difficult and uncertain. Changing rules and regulations as well as customer, employee and stakeholder expectations have resulted in, and are likely to continue to result in, increased general and administrative expenses and increased management time and attention spent complying with or meeting such regulations and expectations. For examp

General Risks Catastrophic events and the effects of climate change, pandemics or other unexpected events may disrupt our business and harm our operating results. Due to the global nature of our business, our operating results may be negatively impacted by catastrophic events and the effects of climate change, pandemics or other unexpected events throughout the world. We rely on a global network of infrastructure applications, enterprise applications and technology systems for our development, marketing, operational, support and sales activities. A disruption or failure of these systems in the event of a major earthquake, fire, extreme temperatures, drought, flood, telecommunications failure, cybersecurity attack, terrorist attack, epidemic or pandemic, or other catastrophic or climate change-related events could cause system interruptions, delays in our product development and loss of c

The increase in cash provided by operating activities for fiscal 2025 compared to fiscal 2024 was primarily due to higher accounts receivable collections, partially offset by lower net income of $902.7 million, the unrealized loss from settlement of the interest rate treasury lock of $121.6 million in fiscal 2025 and higher disbursements for operations, including vendor and tax payments . Cash Provided by (Used in) Investing Activities Net cash used in investing activities was $15.9 billion for fiscal 2025 compared to net cash provided by investing activities of $1.2 billion for fiscal 2024.
